name: Madeiran Portuguese

allophone_rules.notes: The diagnostic Madeiran palatalisation of an intervocalic /l/ to [ʎ] after /i/: quilo -> [ˈkiʎu], mochila -> [muˈʃiʎɐ], vila -> [ˈviʎɐ]. Conditioned on the preceding lattice slot's phoneme being /i/ and the following grapheme being a vowel (so the /l/ is an onset between /i/ and another vowel), which is exactly the environment reported for the feature and correctly leaves an /l/ after other front vowels untouched (teleférico -> [tɨlɨˈfɛɾiku], /l/ after reduced [ɨ]). Source: Segura (2013: §on the Madeiran dialect).

notes: Madeiran Portuguese (Funchal / Ribeira Brava reference). Modelled features: (1) NASAL DIPHTHONG -> NASAL+N — the salient productive Madeiran pattern in the plural/nasal endings -ões -> [õns], -ães -> [ɐ̃ns], -ãos -> [ɐ̃ns] (cães -> [kɐ̃ns]); encoded as multi-character grapheme overrides. (2) INTERVOCALIC /l/ PALATALISATION after /i/ -> [ʎ] — the MAD_L_PALATALISATION allophone rule (quilo -> [ˈkiʎu], mochila -> [muˈʃiʎɐ], vila -> [ˈviʎɐ]). (3) The /v/ ~ /b/ distinction is preserved — Madeiran, like standard European Portuguese and unlike the northern mainland, has NO betacism, so /v/ stays [v] (visitar, levada, novo); the single gold datum vinho -> [ˈbiɲu] is a lexicalised item, not a productive merger, and is deliberately not modelled. NOT MODELLED (documented limits, not faked): singular -ão -> [õns] appears sporadically in the gold (verão -> [vɨˈɾõns]) but the far more frequent monosyllable são keeps [sɐ̃w], so a blanket -ão override would regress the base and only the productive plural/-ães/-ões pattern is encoded; the reported stressed-/i/ diphthongisation ([i] -> [ɐj]) is left out because this gold keeps [i] (dia -> [ˈdiɐ], ilha -> [ˈiʎɐ]) and a blanket rule would regress it — it is a lexically-restricted process best left to a downstream engine; r-diphthongisation (correr -> [kuˈʁweɾ]) and Filipa -> [ˈfʎipɐ] are lexical items; stop aspiration and final devoicing are narrow sub-phonemic detail outside the base scope. Dark coda /l/ [ɫ] and Lisbon-type coda sibilants [ʃ]/[ʒ] are inherited from the pt-PT base allophone rules. NATIVE-SPEAKER VALIDATION (Portuguese With Leo, 'Sotaque e expressões da Madeira', with João Francisco Cunha, funchalense, https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=azx_3sbqwEA): strongly confirms the two modelled deltas — the '-il -> -ilh' /l/->[ʎ] after /i/ ('sempre que há um -i antes de um -l ... acrescentamos o som de lhe ao -l', quilómetro -> 'quilhómetro') and the nasal-diphthong -> nasal+N reduction ('vários ditongos, como -ões ... leões -> leôns', 'cães -> câns', '-e desaparece'). Also attests, LEFT UNMODELLED, the extreme final-vowel reduction/mutation (sentimento -> 'sentmente' / 'sentmentn', bilhardeiro -> 'blhardeire', -oi- syncope 'depois -> depôs') as a gradient/lexical process, and the heavier gerund preference (morphosyntactic). DEFERRED ENRICHMENT: the 8-accents overview (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=pitj0XxYO7I) reports the Madeiran 'coâlho' (stressed <e> -> [ɐ] before a palatal, as in Lisbon) — the island-distinguishing contrast with the São Miguel 'coêlho' [e] (see pt-PT-x-sao-miguel) — but this Madeira deep-dive does not itself demonstrate it and adding the pre-palatal centralisation rule would move the scored ep_dialects/clup_dialect Madeira rows; it is left unmodelled pending a measured before/after pass rather than added blind.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Madeiran diverges in the nasal-diphthong -> nasal+N reduction and the intervocalic /l/ -> [ʎ] palatalisation after /i/, and otherwise inherits the EP reduction and coda allophony.

sources.author: Segura, Luísa
sources.title: Variedades dialetais do português europeu
sources.publisher: In E. B. P. Raposo et al. (eds.), Gramática do Português, vol. I, cap. 3, 85-142, Fundação Calouste Gulbenkian, Lisboa
sources.notes: Standard reference chapter on European Portuguese dialect variation; describes the Madeiran intervocalic /l/ -> [ʎ] palatalisation and the nasal-diphthong patterns of the Atlantic islands.

sources.author: Mateus, M.H.M. & d'Andrade, E.
sources.title: The Phonology of Portuguese
sources.notes: Reference phonology for the inherited European Portuguese reduction, dark coda /l/ [ɫ] and coda sibilants, and the /v/ ~ /b/ contrast.

sources.author: Portuguese With Leo (with João Francisco Cunha, funchalense)
sources.title: Sotaque e expressões da Madeira
sources.notes: Native-speaker Madeira (Funchal) deep-dive (NOT academic). Validates the modelled /l/->[ʎ] after /i/ ('-il -> -ilh', quilómetro->'quilhómetro') and the nasal-diphthong->nasal+N reduction (leões->'leôns', cães->'câns'); documents the unmodelled extreme final-vowel reduction (sentimento->'sentmente') and the deferred Madeiran 'coâlho' pre-palatal centralisation (see notes).

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam). DIPHTHONGS: declared so the bundled syllabifier counts nuclei correctly — without them every vowel run merges into one nucleus and co-e-lho is read as coe-lho, one syllable short, putting the stress and its vowel reduction on the wrong vowel. ⟨oe⟩ is hiatus, not a diphthong (Cunha & Cintra, §Ditongos).

location.notes: Madeira (Funchal reference) — the autonomous region the variety is named for. [Wikidata Q26253 P625]
